localization.notes: Daily and weekly entries are authored as complete copy units so future locale feeds can restate tone without splitting rhetoric into fragments.

promotionPlaybook.purpose: Give teammates a copy-and-paste brief for event, deal, discount, or service promotion.
promotionPlaybook.note: Use the playbook when a promotion needs to be explained to someone who does not know the site well enough to improvise the structure.

event.structure: lead with what it is, when it happens, and why the room matters
event.proof: speaker, venue, time, and who else it is for

deal.goal: Make the offer legible and time-bound
deal.structure: state the deal, the savings, and the deadline in the first pass
deal.riskReversal: simple exit or low-friction next step

discount.goal: Reduce hesitation with a concrete savings frame
discount.structure: show the original price, the discount, and the net result
discount.proof: exact amount saved and eligible audience
discount.riskReversal: clear terms and a no-surprise promise

service.goal: Turn a service into an understandable next step
service.structure: state the outcome, the method, and the first conversation
service.proof: examples, scope, and a concise proof point
service.riskReversal: what happens if the fit is not right

promo.label: Daily promo
promo.title: Help the next release land
promo.summary: Releases are scheduled for the 13th and 26th of each month. Small support keeps the work moving and makes the next page easier to ship.
promo.why: A direct contribution keeps the monthly cadence steady.

promotion.audience: people deciding whether to support the work
promotion.offer: Help the release cadence stay dependable
promotion.proof: The public cadence ships on the 13th and 26th.
promotion.objection: It should be obvious what support changes.
promotion.urgency: The next release window is already on the calendar.

wonder.label: Daily wonder
wonder.title: What changes if the page has a reason to return tomorrow?
wonder.summary: A site can feel alive when one small surface changes on a schedule. This slot is here to make that return legible.

promo.label: Daily promo
promo.title: Package the work as a readable offer
promo.summary: If you are hiring, commissioning, or comparing, the page should say what happens next without making people decode it.

promotion.audience: people deciding whether the service fits the current problem
promotion.offer: A readable way to understand the work before a call
promotion.proof: The service page already names the routes, artifacts, and support path.
promotion.objection: It should be obvious what happens after the first click.
promotion.urgency: A clear decision path reduces the chance of stalling.

wonder.label: Daily wonder
wonder.title: Which route feels like a doorway instead of a destination?
wonder.summary: Some pages are anchors, some are invitations, and some are thresholds. This slot gives the threshold a name.

promo.label: Daily promo
promo.title: Make collaboration easy to imagine
promo.summary: Keep the call to action low friction: a card, a button, a short route, or a clear email path to follow.

promotion.audience: people who want a quick first conversation
promotion.offer: A direct opening for collaboration without heavy ceremony
promotion.proof: The contact route stays plain and open across the site.
promotion.objection: It should be easy to understand the next step.
promotion.urgency: A quick reply path lowers the cost of saying yes.

wonder.label: Daily wonder
wonder.title: Who would be surprised by the breadth of this site?
wonder.summary: Developers, illustrators, authors, and executives all need different cues. A rotating surface can bridge those expectations.

promo.label: Daily promo
promo.title: Point to the tool that reduces effort
promo.summary: The right entry point is often a small utility, a clear route, or a sample that lowers the cost of starting.

wonder.label: Daily wonder
wonder.title: What if the site behaved like a well-run studio?
wonder.summary: The interface can express rhythm, attention, and stewardship without pretending everything is static.

promo.label: Daily promo
promo.title: Offer an entry path for a serious first conversation
promo.summary: The first answer should help someone decide whether they are in the right place before they spend more time.

wonder.label: Daily wonder
wonder.title: Which part of the site is a living specimen today?
wonder.summary: A daily change makes the surface feel maintained, not merely published.

promo.label: Daily promo
promo.title: Keep the work visible enough to trust
promo.summary: People tend to trust a site more when the structure says what it is doing and what it is for.

wonder.label: Daily wonder
wonder.title: What does this week want to teach?
wonder.summary: A weekly frame can carry a larger theme, while daily details keep the site fresh and human.

promo.label: Daily promo
promo.title: Give people a place to begin and a reason to stay
promo.summary: A homepage can do both: point to useful work and create enough atmosphere to invite a second look.

wonder.label: Daily wonder
wonder.title: What kind of collaborator would this site attract?
wonder.summary: The answer should be broader than one discipline. The structure can help people imagine themselves inside it.

promo.label: Weekly promo
promo.title: Keep the release rhythm steady
promo.summary: Development costs are around $250 per month. If the work is useful, direct support keeps the public cadence open.
promo.why: One steady contribution helps keep releases on the 13th and 26th.

promotion.audience: people assessing whether to fund the work
promotion.offer: A transparent way to keep the release rhythm steady
promotion.proof: The work costs about $250 per month to keep moving.
promotion.objection: It should be easy to know where the money goes.
promotion.urgency: The next release cadence depends on steady support.

wonder.label: Weekly wonder
wonder.title: What structure would make this week feel worth revisiting?
wonder.summary: Weekly wonder should feel like a theme, not a slogan.

promo.label: Weekly promo
promo.title: Make the work legible to a new teammate quickly
promo.summary: Good surfaces reduce onboarding time for developers, illustrators, and authors alike.

promotion.audience: people comparing a new route before they commit time
promotion.offer: A faster way to understand the software surface
promotion.proof: The topic register and operator atlas already organize the path.
promotion.objection: The first pass should feel legible, not clever.
promotion.urgency: A better map reduces the cost of onboarding this week.

wonder.label: Weekly wonder
wonder.title: Which gap in career breadth is the site ready to bridge?
wonder.summary: The site can welcome a person with senior relationships and missing context without shaming the gap.

promo.label: Weekly promo
promo.title: Show the starter kit for the decentralized team
promo.summary: Offer routes and tools that help someone imagine joining the work without a heavy ceremony.

wonder.label: Weekly wonder
wonder.title: What would a calm, premium, living homepage look like by Friday?
wonder.summary: The answer can change with the week while the shell stays stable.

promo.label: Weekly promo
promo.title: Turn curiosity into a clear conversation
promo.summary: A good weekly card can invite work, not merely attention.

wonder.label: Weekly wonder
wonder.title: What part of the site should feel newly alive this week?
wonder.summary: This is the place for a slightly stranger question than the daily slot.
